title: Microsoft Business Solutions - The New Wave at Stampede
author: Katherine Jones
source_file: 2002 Microsoft Business Solutions - The New Wave at Stampede.pdf
abstract: At the Stampede partner conference Microsoft Business Solutions (MBS) unveiled a roadmap for business solutions infrastructure targeting small to midsize enterprises (SMEs). The study examines MBS's 'surround' strategy placing business-specific applications atop the business platform, and covers CRM, professional services automation (PSA), and manufacturing applications under Great Plains, Navision, and Salomon product lines. Aberdeen identifies the challenge of owning the full business 'stack' against established ISVs and competing ERP vendors.
importance_rationale: Early authoritative analysis of Microsoft's SME ERP strategy at a pivotal moment when MBS had just acquired both Great Plains (2001) and Navision (2002), shaping the eventual Microsoft Dynamics platform.
relevance_rationale: Microsoft Dynamics 365 (the evolution of MBS) remains a leading SME ERP/CRM platform; the competitive dynamics analyzed — surround strategy and VAR channel — are still highly relevant for studying Microsoft's enterprise software trajectory.
prescience_rationale: Microsoft's surround strategy proved prescient: MBS evolved into Microsoft Dynamics, became a dominant SME ERP player, and the stack ownership challenge was largely resolved by cloud migration to Dynamics 365 by 2016.
